justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Rolling deployment race condition at Step 4 clearly identified — instances serving different read sources during rolling update, same user getting inconsistent results. (2) Missing backfill validation before Step 4 explicitly flagged as 'broken' with no row count check, no completeness flag, no integrity comparison. (3) Non-resumable backfill identified with specific recommendation for cursor-based checkpoint tracking. (4) All three specific recommendations present: validation query before Step 4, feature flag for atomic read-source switch, and resumable backfill with progress tracking. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Identified the backfill-vs-dual-write race condition (stale data overwriting newer dual-writes) — this goes beyond the rubric's expected findings and is a genuine additional data-loss risk
Excellent granularity on the rolling deploy race: explained exactly how load-balanced requests cause preferences to appear/disappear between requests for the same user
Identified the narrowing rollback window at Step 5 — a subtle operational risk not in the rubric but highly relevant
Provided actionable SQL-level recommendations (INSERT ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING, upsert-if-not-exists) rather than vague guidance
Comprehensive observability review identifying zero monitoring across all steps, with specific gaps per migration phase
Escalations are well-routed — architecture issues to MJ, implementation gaps to Shaq
Identified malformed JSON handling in backfill as an edge case requiring per-row error handling and manual remediation

trials.justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Rolling deployment race condition at Step 4 clearly identified — split-brain reads where some instances read from old table and others from new table. (2) Missing backfill validation before Step 4 identified — no completeness check before cutover. (3) Non-resumable backfill identified as a risk — crash at row 500k requires full restart, recommends cursor-based progress tracking. (4) Specific recommendations provided: validation query (COUNT comparison as Step 3.5), feature flag for atomic read-source switching (PREFERENCES_READ_SOURCE), and resumable backfill (cursor-based with last_processed_user_id). (5) Verdict is NOT READY. Additionally, Pippen went beyond the rubric by identifying the backfill-overwrites-dual-write race condition (a genuine additional bug) and providing comprehensive observability gaps.

observations.text: Identified the Step 4 rolling deploy race condition with precise detail — user-visible inconsistency depending on which instance serves the request
Flagged missing validation gate between backfill and read switch, recommending it as an explicit Step 3.5
Identified non-resumable backfill with concrete recommendation: cursor-based progress tracking with last_processed_user_id
All three specific recommendations present: validation query, feature flag (PREFERENCES_READ_SOURCE=old|new), resumable backfill
Found an additional real bug not in the rubric: backfill can overwrite fresher dual-written values without ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
Comprehensive observability review identifying zero monitoring across all migration steps
Escalations are well-routed to appropriate team members with clear reasoning
Verdict is correctly NOT READY with no overcorrection — recommends specific fixes rather than halting the migration

trials.justification: All five pass criteria met: (1) Rolling deployment race condition at Step 4 clearly identified — instances reading from different sources during rolling deploy. (2) Missing backfill validation before Step 4 identified — no check that backfill completed before switching reads. (3) Non-resumable backfill identified as a risk with specific escalation. (4) Specific recommendations provided: validation query (SELECT COUNT), feature flag (READ_PREFERENCES_SOURCE), resumable backfill with cursor-based checkpointing. (5) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Identified the backfill race condition (stale data overwriting dual-written data) which goes beyond the rubric requirements — a genuine additional finding
Provided concrete SQL for validation query and specific INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING fix for backfill safety
Feature flag recommendation is well-specified with exact env var name and values (READ_PREFERENCES_SOURCE=legacy|new)
Comprehensive observability review identifying zero monitoring coverage across all migration steps
Escalations are well-routed to appropriate team members with clear rationale
Identified dual-write transactional integrity as an additional risk not in the rubric
